* Sounder North runs peak weekdays only (~4 trains each direction) — no weekend service. † Property tax estimates based on ~0.85% effective rate (Snohomish Co.) and ~1.05% (King Co.) applied to median home price; actual bills vary by assessed value and levies. ‡ Crime Index: relative scale 1–100 (lower = safer). National average ≈ 47. Based on NeighborhoodScout/AreaVibes estimates; ⭐ = among safest in King/Snohomish County. § Price-to-Rent: median home ÷ annual 2BR rent — below 20 favors buying, above 25 favors renting. Airport times are off-peak driving estimates.

🚌 Commute to Downtown Seattle

Sort by

Lynnwood

🚇 Link Light Rail35–40 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~25 min
🚗 Drive (peak)55–75 min

Lynnwood City Center Link station opened Aug 2024. Trains every 8–12 min peak.

Renton

🚌 Metro Bus (express)45–70 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~25 min
🚗 Drive (peak)45–60 min

No direct light rail. Drive or bus to Rainier Beach / Tukwila Intl Blvd for Link. Routes 101, 102, 169 serve Seattle.

Edmonds

🚂 Sounder Train~40 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~30 min
🚗 Drive (peak)55–70 min

Sounder: ~4 trains/day peak weekdays only — no weekends. Ferry goes to Kingston (Kitsap), not Seattle.

Issaquah

🚌 ST 554 Express Bus40–55 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~25 min
🚗 Drive (peak)45–60 min

ST 554 uses I-90 HOV lanes — one of the most reliable suburban express routes. Issaquah Link is planned but years away.

Shoreline

🚇 Link Light Rail20–30 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~15 min
🚗 Drive (peak)30–45 min

Two Link stations opened 2024: Shoreline South/148th & Shoreline North/185th. Fastest transit commute of all 9 cities.

Maple Valley

🚌 Metro Bus (routes 153/168)65–90 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~35 min
🚗 Drive (peak)55–70 min

Worst transit access of the nine. Most residents drive. SR-169 → I-405 → I-90 to downtown.

E. Renton Highlands

🚌 Metro Bus55–75 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~30 min
🚗 Drive (peak)50–65 min

Unincorporated plateau east of Renton. Bus connects to Renton TC then express. Mostly car-dependent.

Mukilteo

🚂 Sounder Train~50 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~30 min
🚗 Drive (peak)50–65 min

Sounder North: ~4 trains/day peak weekdays. Community Transit also connects to Lynnwood City Center Link station.

Everett

🚂 Sounder / ST Express 51260–75 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~35 min
🚗 Drive (peak)55–75 min

Everett Station: Sounder North + ST Express 512 to Seattle. Link rail extension planned ~2037.

Bothell

🚌 ST Express 522 / CT 87145–55 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~25 min
🚗 Drive (peak)45–60 min

SR-522 BRT (Stride Line 2) opening 2026 — will connect to Mountlake Terrace Link station.

Woodinville

🚌 ST Express 522 / CT 93155–65 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~25 min
🚗 Drive (peak)45–60 min

No direct Link access. Stride BRT (2026) to Mountlake Terrace or drive to Bothell P&R for 522 express.

North Bend

🚌 Metro Bus 208 (to Issaquah TC)90+ min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~40 min
🚗 Drive (peak)60–80 min

Essentially car-dependent. I-90 can seize completely during peak — worst traffic exposure on this list.

Mercer Island

🚇 Link Light Rail (2 Line)15–20 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~15 min
🚗 Drive (peak)25–40 min

Mercer Island station on the 2 Line (East Link) opened April 2023 — one of the fastest transit commutes to Seattle on this list.

Newcastle

🚌 Metro Bus (indirect via Renton)50–70 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~25 min
🚗 Drive (peak)40–55 min

Effectively car-dependent. I-405 or SR-169 southbound to I-90 westbound to downtown. No direct transit option.

Mountlake Terrace

🚇 Link Light Rail30–38 min
🚗 Drive (off-peak)~20 min
🚗 Drive (peak)45–60 min

Mountlake Terrace Link station opened Aug 2024 (Lynnwood extension). SR-522 Stride BRT (~2026) will connect from Bothell, greatly improving east-west access.

🏙️ City Deep Dives

Sort by

Lynnwood Snohomish County · ~16 mi N of Seattle · Pop. ~43,000

Median Home~$635K
2BR Rent~$2,350

Pros

  • Direct Link Light Rail to Seattle (2024)
  • Most affordable north-I-5 option
  • Alderwood Mall — major retail hub
  • Growing city center near rail station
  • Near Mukilteo waterfront

Cons

  • I-5 among worst corridors in WA state
  • Strip-mall heavy — limited charm
  • Moderate crime vs. Edmonds/Issaquah
  • Limited walkability off transit core
  • Uneven neighborhood quality

🏙️ Transit-Oriented Suburb

Renton King County · ~11 mi SE of Seattle · Pop. ~107,000

Median Home~$695K
2BR Rent~$2,450

Pros

  • Most diverse food & cultural scene
  • Gene Coulon Beach Park — Lake Washington
  • The Landing — open-air retail & dining
  • Sea-Tac Airport ~15 min away
  • Boeing + Eastside tech employers nearby

Cons

  • No direct light rail — bus/drive to Link
  • I-405 consistently gridlocked at peak
  • Uneven neighborhoods (north vs. south)
  • Downtown core has higher crime pockets
  • Less charming than Edmonds/Issaquah

🌮 Diverse Working-Class City

Edmonds Snohomish County · ~15 mi N of Seattle · Pop. ~42,000

Median Home~$810K
2BR Rent~$2,600

Pros

  • Charming walkable downtown & farmers market
  • Puget Sound waterfront — Olympic views
  • Very safe — low crime throughout
  • World-class scuba diving (Underwater Park)
  • Sounder train + strong community identity

Cons

  • Premium prices — Snohomish's most expensive
  • Very tight rental inventory
  • Sounder: ~4 trains/day, no weekends
  • Ferry goes to Kingston — NOT Seattle
  • Skews older demographic; limited nightlife

⛵ Charming Coastal Small Town

Issaquah King County · ~17 mi E of Seattle · Pop. ~40,000

Median Home~$940K
2BR Rent~$2,800

Pros

  • Top-rated schools in Washington State
  • Cascades trailheads in your backyard
  • Snoqualmie Pass skiing ~30 min away
  • Very safe — lowest crime of all nine
  • Charming old town (Front Street)
  • ST 554 via I-90 HOV — reliably fast

Cons

  • Most expensive of all nine cities
  • No light rail (planned, years away)
  • Car-dependent outside Highlands & old town
  • I-90 eastbound evening backup severe
  • Demographically homogeneous / "bubble" feel

🏔️ Affluent Family Suburb

Shoreline King County · ~8 mi N of Seattle · Pop. ~55,000

Median Home~$720K
2BR Rent~$2,325

Pros

  • Closest suburb — just 8 miles from downtown
  • Two new Link stations (2024): 20–30 min to Seattle
  • More affordable than Seattle proper
  • Richmond Beach Saltwater Park — Sound views
  • Ronald Bog & Paramount Parks nearby
  • Growing development around both rail stations

Cons

  • Limited distinct downtown / city-center identity
  • Car-dependent away from the two rail stations
  • Moderate crime — Aurora Ave corridor issues
  • Housing inventory tightening near new Link stations
  • Limited dining/nightlife relative to Seattle proximity

🌉 Gateway to Seattle

Maple Valley King County · ~22 mi SE of Seattle · Pop. ~27,000

Median Home~$760K
2BR Rent~$2,425

Pros

  • Excellent Tahoma School District (top WA)
  • Very safe — low crime family community
  • Lake Wilderness Park & Aquatic Center
  • Maple Valley Trail system for cycling/hiking
  • More affordable than Issaquah/Bellevue
  • Strong small-town community feel

Cons

  • Worst transit access — nearly zero viable options
  • Long peak commute (SR-169 → I-405 congestion)
  • Very car-dependent — minimal walkability
  • No direct highway to downtown Seattle
  • Bedroom community — limited employment base

🌲 Quiet Exurban Family Community

East Renton Highlands King County (unincorp.) · ~20 mi SE of Seattle · Pop. ~14,000

Median Home~$650K
2BR Rent~$2,225

Pros

  • One of the more affordable King County options
  • Quiet residential plateau above Renton
  • Access to Renton amenities & The Landing
  • Less dense than urban Renton
  • Close to SE Seattle employment corridors

Cons

  • Unincorporated — no city services or identity
  • Very car-dependent — poor transit coverage
  • Limited local amenities (depends on Renton)
  • Below-average schools (Renton SD)
  • "In-between" feel — not Renton, not Issaquah

🏘️ Affordable Plateau Suburb

Mukilteo Snohomish County · ~25 mi N of Seattle · Pop. ~22,000

Median Home~$760K
2BR Rent~$2,425

Pros

  • Charming Puget Sound waterfront community
  • Excellent Mukilteo School District
  • Boeing Everett campus — major employer
  • Paine Field regional airport nearby
  • Community Transit connects to Lynnwood Link
  • Ferry to Whidbey Island for weekend trips

Cons

  • Sounder: ~4 trains/day, peak weekdays only
  • Ferry goes to Whidbey Island — not Seattle
  • I-5 peak traffic can be very slow northbound
  • More car-dependent than Lynnwood or Shoreline
  • Premium prices for Snohomish County

◆ 50% midpoint
⚓ Waterfront Boeing Community

Everett Snohomish County · ~30 mi N of Seattle · Pop. ~115,000

Median Home~$515K
2BR Rent~$2,000

Pros

  • Most affordable of all nine cities
  • Sounder + ST Express 512 transit options
  • Revitalized waterfront (Port Gardner)
  • Boeing Paine Field — largest employer in region
  • Large city — full amenities, diverse dining
  • Near Tulalip Resort & outlets

Cons

  • Longest commute to Seattle of all nine
  • Some neighborhoods have significant crime
  • Link light rail not expected until ~2037
  • Industrial/working-class feel vs. Edmonds/Issaquah
  • I-5 through Everett is frequently congested

◆ 50% midpoint
🏭 Most Affordable — Largest Snohomish City

Bothell King/Snohomish County · ~15 mi NE of Seattle · Pop. ~55,000

Median Home~$850K
2BR Rent~$2,700

Pros

  • Northshore School District — top-rated in WA state
  • Revitalized walkable downtown with dining & waterfront
  • Sammamish River Trail & Burke-Gilman Trail access
  • UW Bothell campus — university-town energy
  • SR-522 BRT (Stride) opening 2026 — major transit upgrade
  • Low crime, family-friendly neighborhoods

Cons

  • Among the priciest suburbs on this list
  • No light rail yet — BRT connects to Link, not direct
  • SR-522 & I-405 heavily congested peak hours
  • Car-dependent outside downtown core
  • Rapid growth straining infrastructure

◆ 50% midpoint
🎓 Top Schools — Revitalized Downtown

Woodinville King County · ~17 mi NE of Seattle · Pop. ~14,000

Median Home~$1.1M
2BR Rent~$3,050

Pros

  • Wine & brewery district — 100+ tasting rooms
  • Northshore SD — top-rated schools in WA state
  • Very safe, low crime, peaceful suburban feel
  • Sammamish River Trail & Burke-Gilman access
  • Large lots, more space vs. closer-in suburbs
  • Near Redmond/Bellevue tech corridor (short drive)

Cons

  • Most expensive on this list — median over $1M
  • No light rail; transit is slow and indirect
  • Extremely car-dependent — minimal walkability
  • SR-522 heavily congested during peak hours
  • Limited urban amenities — small-town feel only

🍷 Wine Country — Premium Suburban Living

North Bend King County · ~30 mi SE of Seattle · Pop. ~9,000

Median Home~$750K
2BR Rent~$2,350

Pros

  • Mt. Si, Rattlesnake Ledge, Twin Falls — world-class hiking
  • Snoqualmie Pass skiing ~30 min away
  • Very safe — one of the lowest crime rates in King County
  • Snoqualmie River & valley scenery
  • Premium Outlets mall + Salish Lodge nearby
  • More affordable than Issaquah/Woodinville

Cons

  • Farthest from Seattle — worst commute on this list
  • I-90 can be completely gridlocked peak hours
  • No meaningful transit — fully car-dependent
  • Very limited local amenities and nightlife
  • Flood & wildfire risk in surrounding areas

🏔️ Hiking Paradise — Cascade Gateway

Mercer Island King County · ~4 mi E of Seattle · Pop. ~25,000

Median Home~$1.65M
2BR Rent~$3,750

Pros

  • Direct Link Light Rail (2 Line) — 15–20 min to Seattle
  • Top-rated Mercer Island School District
  • Surrounded by Lake Washington — stunning water views
  • Extremely safe — one of the lowest crime rates in King County
  • I-90 HOV lanes — fast off-peak drive to Seattle or Bellevue
  • Strong community identity, excellent parks and trails

Cons

  • Among the most expensive suburbs in the state
  • Only accessible via I-90 — no alternate routes on/off island
  • I-90 severely congested during peak hours
  • Very car-dependent outside transit corridor
  • Limited retail — depends on Seattle or Bellevue for shopping

🏝️ Affluent Island Enclave

Newcastle King County · ~13 mi SE of Seattle · Pop. ~13,000

Median Home~$950K
2BR Rent~$2,650

Pros

  • Quiet hilltop community with newer construction
  • Lake Boren Park — sandy beach, swimming, fishing, trails
  • Coal Creek Natural Area — extensive forested trail system
  • Close to Bellevue tech corridor (10–15 min drive)
  • Very safe — low crime throughout
  • Higher-quality housing stock at lower cost than Bellevue

Cons

  • No meaningful transit — fully car-dependent
  • No real downtown or walkable commercial center
  • Schools primarily in Renton SD (mixed quality)
  • Hilly terrain makes biking/walking impractical
  • I-405 and SR-169 congestion affects all commute routes

🏡 Quiet Hilltop Near Bellevue

Mountlake Terrace Snohomish County · ~13 mi N of Seattle · Pop. ~22,000

Median Home~$670K
2BR Rent~$2,325

Pros

  • Direct Link Light Rail station (opened Aug 2024)
  • More affordable than Shoreline and Lynnwood
  • SR-522 Stride BRT (~2026) connecting from Bothell
  • Ballinger Lake & Park — swimming, boating, fishing
  • I-5 access north and south; close to Lynnwood hub
  • Diverse community with improving local amenities

Cons

  • I-5 among worst corridors in state during peak hours
  • Limited walkable commercial district
  • Schools rated average (Edmonds SD)
  • Less established identity than Shoreline or Edmonds
  • Apartment/condo heavy — tight single-family inventory

🌿 Rural 2% Urban · Rural Split 98% Urban 🏙️
◆ 50% midpoint
🚇 Emerging Transit Hub

Stockton San Joaquin County, CA · Central Valley · Pop. ~320,000

Median Home~$380K
2BR Rent~$1,725

Pros

  • One of California's most affordable cities
  • Large, diverse population & food scene
  • San Joaquin Delta — boating & waterways
  • University of the Pacific campus
  • Improving downtown revitalization efforts
  • Central location between Bay Area & Sacramento

Cons

  • Extreme summers — 95°F+ heat common July–Sept
  • Poor air quality (Central Valley inversion)
  • Bay Area commute ~1.5–2 hrs each way
  • Minimal public transit — car-dependent
  • Limited high-wage local employment

🚆 Future Transit Timeline

✅ Completed
Apr 2023
East Link (2 Line) Opens
Light rail extended across Lake Washington — direct rail from Mercer Island and Bellevue to downtown Seattle, trains every 8–12 min peak
Mercer Island
Aug 2024
Lynnwood City Center Link
Light rail extended north — direct rail to downtown Seattle, trains every 8–12 min peak
Lynnwood Mountlake Terrace
Aug 2024
Shoreline Link (×2 stations)
Shoreline South/148th & Shoreline North/185th open — 20–30 min to Seattle, fastest on this list
Shoreline
🟡 ~2026
~2026
Stride BRT Line 2 (SR-522)
Bus Rapid Transit: Shoreline → Kenmore → Bothell → Mountlake Terrace Link station. Replaces ST 522 express with faster, more frequent service.
Bothell Woodinville
🔵 ~2028–2031
~2029
Stride BRT Line 1 (I-405)
BRT along I-405 corridor from Lynnwood south through Bellevue to Renton — major eastside spine connecting several suburbs to Link
Renton Lynnwood
🔮 ~2035–2037
~2035
Issaquah Link Spur
Light rail: Issaquah → South Kirkland P&R → Bellevue downtown. Funding & alignment under review in ST3 realignment.
Issaquah
~2037
Everett Link Extension
Link extended from Lynnwood north through Mountlake Terrace to Everett Station — will dramatically cut the 60–75 min transit commute
Everett

Dates are Sound Transit ST3 projections, subject to change. Source: soundtransit.org

Data reflects mid-2026 market conditions. Prices are estimates — verify on Zillow/Redfin before making decisions.

Commute times are estimates; transit times assume off-peak. Demographics from ACS 2022 estimates.

Static data verified: 2026-06-05  ·  Live weather fetched on demand via wttr.in

📋 Data Sources & Methodology
Housing prices & $/sqft: Zillow / Redfin market reports
Rental rates: Zillow Rental Index / ApartmentList
Median income: U.S. Census ACS 5-Year (2022)
GreatSchools ratings: GreatSchools.org district averages (2024)
Crime index: NeighborhoodScout / AreaVibes estimates
Transit times: Sound Transit / King County Metro schedules
Weather averages: NOAA Climate Normals (1991–2020)
Live weather: wttr.in — fetched on demand, cached 6 hrs locally
Property tax: ~0.85% (Snohomish Co.) / ~1.05% (King Co.) effective rates
Home sizes (sqft): Zillow / Redfin median sold size estimates
Stale data warning: Auto-shown after 180 days from verification date
All data: Estimates only — verify before major financial decisions
